fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable and trading at a very high forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — significant future growth expected.
Forward P/E 39.58x, PEG 0.78x — growth is priced in, but PEG suggests value if estimates hold.
Current ratio 3.83, Quick ratio 2.85 — strong short-term liquidity.
Total cash $439.70M vs Total debt $280.42M — manageable leverage.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6%, Profit margin -8.5% — company is not profitable on an operating or net basis.
Return on equity -6.1%, Return on assets -3.3% — poor returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Trailing P/E n/a — not profitable on a trailing basis.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing strong demand from data centers and AI computing, highlighted by a recent large upgrade deal with Mediacom. However, the stock saw a significant pullback recently, alongside other optics players, suggesting a cooling-off period after a substantial run-up. Despite the volatility, some analysts remain bullish, seeing potential for continued growth, though valuation and profitability remain key concerns.
